# Context

Explain here the context of the PR: what happened that made you create this PR?

E.g.:
- There were a lot of issues with the X feature that when a user tried to use it in this Y way, it broke and...
- A lot of users are requesting thie X feature and by demand....
- We were having performance problems with this X feature and....

Keep it simple however keep it clear why this is needed.

Make sure you properly label the pull request.

# Proposed resolution

Explain here what you did to implement a feature, solve a bug, remove some unsed or buggy code, etc.

Try to reference files and lines to key parts of your PR here. You could do any of these:
- Replicate the code in a code block (make sure you set the language)
- Attach a screenshot of the said code
- Add a GitHub permalink with the specific lines selected to make it more clear (this is preferred)

# Impact

Explain here what's the impact of this PR.

Make it clear.

Is the impact allowing the users to have a new feature and close X issues?

Is the impact that the application nor feature is performing better X%?

Do you have benchmarks?

# Screenshots (if applicable)

If your PR changes anything on the frontend of the application, add screenshots here.

# Impacted resources

What resources are impacted by your PR?

E.g.:
- Saving screenshots
- Scrolling Capture
- Boot

# Testing/validation

What are the steps to actually test what your PR is doing to properly validate it?

# Rollback plan

Sometimes not everything goes well. While a new patch would be better to fix something that just broke, sometimes a rollback is necessary.

What's the plan?

- Just revert the PR?
- Revert the PR and delete the old release?
etc

# References

Are there any references to your PR?

A card, an article that sustains it, a list of issues?, etc?
